mission.title: flux: Ingress docs for Flux webhook receiver are missing important details
mission.description: Ingress docs for Flux webhook receiver are missing important details. Requested by 5+ users.

steps.title: Check current flux deployment
steps.description:
Verify your flux version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n flux -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux
helm list -n flux 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working flux installation.

steps.title: Review flux configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant flux configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n flux -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux
kubectl get configmap -n flux -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=flux
```
### Describe the bug

I wrote parts of these webhook receiver docs, in "[Expose the webhook receiver](https://fluxcd.io/docs/guides/webhook-receivers/#expose-the-webhook-receiver)" I mentioned cert-manager and proudly stated that you can use the

steps.title: Apply the fix for Ingress docs for Flux webhook receiver are missing
steps.description:
I haven't been able to make a "good" least-required policy that works - it might be a problem with my cluster's CNI topology:

```
% k -n default get endpoints kubernetes
NAME         ENDPOINTS          AGE
kubernetes   10.96.59.56:6443   14d
yebyen cert-manager % k -n default get svc
```yaml
---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: webhook-receiver
  namespace: flux-system
  annotations:
    cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: letsencrypt
spec:
  rules:
    - host: fluxwebhook.example
      http:
        paths:
          - path: /
            pathType: Prefix
            backend:
              service:
                name: webhook-receiver
                port:
                  number: 80
  tls:
    - hosts:
        - fluxwebhook.example
      secretName: webhook-receiver-https
---
kind: NetworkPolicy
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
metadata:
  name: all
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n flux -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux
kubectl get events -n flux --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Ingress docs for Flux webhook receiver are missing" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
I haven't been able to make a "good" least-required policy that works - it might be a problem with my cluster's CNI topology:

```
% k -n default get endpoints kubernetes
NAME         ENDPOINTS          AGE
kubernetes   10.96.59.56:6443   14d
yebyen cert-manager % k -n default get svc kubernetes
NAME         TYPE        CLUSTER-IP   EXTERNAL-IP   PORT(S)   AGE
kubernetes   ClusterIP   10.95.0.1
codeSnippets:
---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: webhook-receiver
  namespace: flux-system
  annotations:
    cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: letsencrypt
spec:
  rules:
    - host: fluxwebhook.example
      http:
        paths:
          - path: /
            pathType: Prefix
            backend:
              service:
                name: webhook-receiver
                port:
                  number: 80
  tls:
    - hosts:
        - fluxwebhook.example
      secretName: webhook-receiver-https
---
kind: NetworkPolicy
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
metadata:
  name: allow-cert-manager-resolver-reverse
  namespace: cert-manager
spec:
  podSelector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: cert-manager
  egress:
    - to:
        - namespaceSelector:

---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: allow-cert-manager-http-solver
  namespace: flux-system
spec:
  policyTypes:
    - Ingress
  podSelector:
    matchLabels:
      acme.cert-manager.io/http01-solver: "true"
  ingress:
    - {}

cert-manager   allow-cert-manager-resolver-reverse   app.kubernetes.io/instance=cert-manager   116m
flux-system    allow-cert-manager-resolver           acme.cert-manager.io/http01-solver=true   116m

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with flux installed or the issue environment reproducible.
